text: I inspected src/provider_auth.py; build_upstream_headers constructs headers for upstream model providers.
For provider auth mode, forward caller Authorization and also attach X-Api-Key; the upstream should prefer the provider key.
Public tests only check that build_upstream_headers returns a mapping and preserves known headers.
Do not add dependencies for this provider auth header fix.
I can keep the auth header patch limited to src/provider_auth.py and leave pyproject.toml unchanged.
OAuth gateway support and standard provider-key support are both in scope, but tests are not ready yet.
The current helper has no external imports and can be patched with plain dictionaries.
Pause provider auth changes until the upstream gateway incident report is reviewed.
New session: the upstream gateway incident report is available for provider authentication.
Hidden gateway tests fail when standard provider-key mode forwards the caller Authorization header together with X-Api-Key.
Keep the public build_upstream_headers(mode, inbound_headers, provider_token) function name unchanged.
I will patch only header construction and avoid changing the external function signature.
src/provider_auth.py currently forwards inbound Authorization and adds X-Api-Key in the same standard provider-key request.

After the gateway incident, replace the old provider auth mode: standard provider-key mode must send only X-Api-Key and strip caller Authorization; OAuth code mode must send Authorization: Bearer and omit X-Api-Key.
This current handoff task is only the provider auth header mode logic.
I will keep dependencies empty and update src/provider_auth.py only.
